--- SOURCE 1: Criteria for Evaluation of Novel Markers of Cardiovascular Risk ---
URL: https://www.ahajournals.org/doi/10.1161/circulationaha.109.192278

SUMMARY:
A novel risk marker should be evaluated in several phases, including initial proof of concept, prospective validation in independent populations.

--- SOURCE 3: AHA names biggest advances in cardiovascular research for 2024 ---
URL: https://www.heart.org/en/around-the-aha/aha-names-biggest-advances-in-cardiovascular-research-for-2024

SUMMARY:
Cardiovascular diseases – including coronary heart disease, heart failure, atrial fibrillation and stroke – are the most common causes of death in the U.S. After decades of reducing CVD and stroke-associated death and disease, that trend has reversed course and prevalence has begun rising again in recent years. The PREVENT risk calculator is a new tool developed by volunteer science experts working with the American Heart Association to help people better understand their risk for developing heart disease, stroke or heart failure. It calculates 10- and 30-year total cardiovascular disease risk using heart, kidney and metabolic health measures such as cholesterol, blood pressure, blood glucose, weight and age.

FULL CONTENT:
Published Time: 2024-12-10

AHA names biggest advances in cardiovascular research for 2024 | American Heart Association

enriched_points.expanded_content: Advancements in cardiovascular interventions should undergo rigorous evaluation phases, including proof of concept and prospective validation in diverse populations to ensure reliability and effectiveness.
Evaluating innovations requires analysis of their direct effects on patient outcomes, focusing on improvements in quality of life, reduction in morbidity, and long-term survival rates.

Innovations must be assessed for their sustainability and potential to reshape cardiovascular care paradigms, influencing future treatment approaches, patient management strategies, and healthcare policies.
